name: Natural Monument Cueva del Gato via the Guadiaro River
name_original: Monumento Natural Cueva del Gato por el Río Guadiaro
region_intro: Hiking around Benaoján offers access to diverse and dramatic natural landscapes within the Serranía de Ronda. The region is characterized by the impressive limestone massif of Sierra de Líbar and the Guadiaro River, creating rugged, karst formations, valleys, and canyons. It is an integral part of the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This geological setting provides varied terrain for hikers, from riverside paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Benaoján The most popular hiking route is Natural Monument Cueva del Gato via the Guadiaro River, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to the distinctive entrance of the Cueva del Gato, said to resemble a cat's eye. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hermitage Pond – Guadiaro River Trail loop from Benaoján, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.4 km) path. This route traces the banks of the Guadiaro River, offering serene views of the water and surrounding nature. Local hikers also love the Charco de la Barranca – Cat Cave loop from Benaojan-Montejaque, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Charco de la Barranca, often completed in about 6 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Benaoján is defined by limestone massifs, the Guadiaro River, and karst formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to demanding multi-hour treks. The routes in Benaoján are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Benaoján's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Benaoján?
A: There are over a dozen hiking routes around Benaoján, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 7 easy trails, 6 moderate, and 3 more challenging options to explore the diverse landscapes of the Serranía de Ronda.

Q: What are the best trails for beginners or families with children?
A: For an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995623612/natural-monument-cueva-del-gato-via-the-guadiaro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Monument Cueva del Gato via the Guadiaro River&lt;/a&gt;. This 5.1 km trail is relatively flat and leads to the distinctive entrance of the Cueva del Gato. Another short, easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave loop from Benaoján&lt;/a&gt;, which is less than a kilometer.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benaoján?
A: Yes, Benaoján offers several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage Pond – Guadiaro River Trail loop from Benaoján&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.4 km long. For a shorter moderate loop, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21830284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave – Charco de la Barranca loop from Benaojan-Montejaque&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 8.9 km. The region also features a circular route through the Llanos de Libar and del Republicano, allowing you to explore the heart of the Sierra de Líbar.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: The trails around Benaoján offer access to several significant natural and historical sites. You can visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/857563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave&lt;/a&gt; (Cueva del Gato), known for its distinctive entrance and underground river system. Another highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5209726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Pileta&lt;/a&gt;, renowned for its Paleolithic cave paintings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/251032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo (New Bridge) in Ronda&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de Ronda (Ronda Gorge)&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach, offering spectacular views.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming or refreshing stops along the trails?
A: Yes, the Guadiaro River is a central feature of the area, and a significant highlight is the "Nacimiento de Benaoján" (Benaoján Spring). Here, underground waters from the Sierra de Líbar emerge to form a serene, clear, and cold lagoon, perfect for a refreshing stop during your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benaoján?
A: The best times for hiking in Benaoján are typically spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking. Winters are generally mild, but some trails might be affected by rain, especially those near the Cueva del Gato, which can be hazardous during heavy rainfall.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Benaoján?
A: Many trails in the Serranía de Ronda and Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas to protect wildlife and other hikers. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved around livestock and other animals you might encounter.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in diverse flora and fauna. While hiking, you might spot griffon vultures, eagles, the Spanish ibex, Bonelli's eagles, peregrine falcons, otters, Egyptian mongooses, and wild boars. The routes through the Sierra de Líbar are also home to holm oaks and Portuguese oaks.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through Benaoján?
A: Yes, Benaoján is part of two significant long-distance routes. It is an integral segment of the GR 7, an extensive long-distance hiking route that traverses Spain. Additionally, the village is a segment of the 25th stage of the Great Malaga Path, offering opportunities for multi-day treks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Benaoján?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic and diverse natural landscapes, the unique karst formations, and the serene paths along the Guadiaro River. The variety of trails, from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain treks, is also a major draw.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Benaoján?
A: Benaoján has a train station (Benaoján-Montejaque) which can be a convenient way to access some trailheads, including routes leading to the Cueva de la Pileta. This allows for linear walks or accessing the village without a car. It's recommended to check local train schedules for planning your trip.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking or visiting caves?
A: While most hiking trails do not require permits, entry into specific natural monuments like the Cueva del Gato can be hazardous during heavy rains and may require appropriate permits and equipment for exploration beyond the entrance. The Cueva de la Pileta can be visited by appointment. It's always best to check with local authorities or visitor centers for the latest requirements, especially for cave exploration.
tips: Formerly it was the natural pool of Benaoján. Bathing is currently allowed and there is an esplanade for resting on the right bank. The quality of the water is regulated by the Ronda treatment plant; however, sometimes bathing is not recommended, since there may be uncontrolled contributions of manure from livestock farms.

This wonderful place has been declared a Natural Monument and with good reason. It is a cave with a natural waterfall that marks the outlet of the Guadiaro river after its channel hides inside the rock, traveling through caves and underground galleries for almost 4 km from the site known as Hundidero del Gato upstream. Here you will also find a picnic area and benches that make it the ideal place for a break and appreciate this wonderful environment. Take care and respect the indications, it is forbidden to enter the cave.
